title: Rich Clay-Pot Vietnamese Pork Stew
description: A hearty, one-pot Vietnamese stew made with slow-cooked pork, sour cream, and herbs.
instructions: Preheat the copper pot over medium-high heat.
Add the coconut oil and brown the pork shoulder on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it's softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, bruised lemongrass, galangal, and bay leaves to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and cover it with a lid.
Transfer the pot to a preheated oven at 300°F (150°C) and braise the stew for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, remove the pot from the oven and stir in the sour cream.
Add the fish sauce to the pot and season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a side of steamed rice or noodles.
Note: If using a clay pot, make sure it's preheated before adding the ingredients.
